description: malloc lifecycle: leak (no free), double free, and use after free patterns

expected.notes: malloc at line 5 never freed in alloc_leak
free called twice on same pointer in double_free
strcpy on buf after free in use_after_free

Deliberately suppressed by Layer D (`is_c_buffer_call_literal_safe` in src/ast.rs): the source argument is a string literal (`"hello"`) so the contributed length is statically bounded — no attacker-controlled overflow vector regardless of `buf`'s size. Positive coverage for the unsuppressed path lives in tests/fixtures/real_world/c/taint/buffer_overflow.c (`strcpy(buf, input)` where input is a parameter).
Suppressed by Layer D: literal source. See line 5 entry for full rationale.

Suppressed by Layer D: literal source `"oops"`. The lifecycle bug at this line (use-after-free) is still flagged by the `state-use-after-close` rule above; Layer D's suppression is specifically for the AST pattern's exploitation surface.
